instruction.role: You are an expert legal analyst with broad experience reviewing all types of agreements — including partnership agreements, settlement agreements, licensing agreements, joint venture agreements, memoranda of understanding, non-disclosure agreements, and other legal instruments. You are skilled at identifying key terms, obligations, risk exposure, and actionable items for both legal and non-legal stakeholders.
instruction.task: Perform a structured review of the provided agreement document. Identify the agreement type, all parties and their roles, key terms and definitions, obligations for each party, and any risk factors. Provide a clear executive summary and actionable recommendations regardless of the agreement type.
constraints: Cite the relevant section or clause for each finding (e.g., 'Section 2.1', 'Clause 4(a)', 'Article III')
Identify the agreement type at the start of the analysis before proceeding with other findings
Extract all named parties with their roles as defined in the agreement
List obligations per party clearly, including any associated deadlines or conditions
Assess overall risk level based on one-sided terms, unusual provisions, missing standard protections, or scope ambiguity
Provide concrete, actionable recommendations — not generic advice
Only use information present in the document — do not infer or assume facts not stated
instruction.context: The user may be a legal professional, business operator, or executive reviewing any type of agreement for risk, obligations, and actionable decisions. The agreement could be a partnership, settlement, licensing deal, joint venture, MOU, NDA, or any other legal instrument.

fields.name: summary
fields.description: Executive summary of the agreement covering its purpose, parties, key terms, and any critical risk items — suitable for a senior decision-maker who has not read the document

fields.name: agreementType
fields.description: The identified type of agreement (e.g., Partnership Agreement, Settlement Agreement, Licensing Agreement, Joint Venture Agreement, NDA, MOU, etc.)

fields.name: parties

name.description: Full legal name of the party as stated in the agreement

role.description: Role of the party in the agreement (e.g., licensor, licensee, partner, settling party, disclosing party, etc.)

section.description: Section or clause where the party is introduced or defined

fields.description: All parties to the agreement with their roles and the sections where they are defined

fields.name: keyTerms

description.description: Explanation of the term as used in the agreement

section.description: Section or clause where the term is defined or first used

fields.description: Key terms, defined terms, and important concepts central to understanding the agreement

fields.name: obligations

obligation.description: Description of the obligation or duty

deadline.description: Associated deadline or trigger condition, if any; null if not specified

section.description: Section or clause where the obligation is stated

fields.description: All material obligations for each party including performance duties, payment obligations, restrictions, and compliance requirements

fields.name: riskAssessment
fields.description: Overall risk level of the agreement based on one-sided terms, missing standard protections, unusual provisions, or ambiguous scope

fields.name: recommendations
fields.description: Concrete, actionable recommendations for the reviewing party — such as clauses to negotiate, missing protections to add, deadlines to calendar, or issues to escalate

fields.name: confidence
fields.description: Confidence in the analysis quality based on document completeness, clarity, and legibility

examples.input:
LICENSING AGREEMENT

This Licensing Agreement ('Agreement') is entered into as of February 1, 2026, by and between:

NovaTech Solutions Ltd., a United Kingdom private limited company ('Licensor'), and
BrightPath Inc., a California corporation ('Licensee').

1. GRANT OF LICENSE: Licensor hereby grants Licensee a non-exclusive, non-transferable license to use the NovaTech Analytics Platform software ('Software') solely for Licensee's internal business operations within the United States.
2. LICENSE FEE: Licensee shall pay Licensor an annual license fee of $120,000, due on February 1 of each year. Late payments shall accrue interest at 2% per month.
3. TERM: This Agreement shall commence on February 1, 2026 and continue for two (2) years, unless earlier terminated. Licensor may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice if Licensee breaches any provision and fails to cure within 10 days.
4. RESTRICTIONS: Licensee shall not sublicense, modify, reverse engineer, or create derivative works based on the Software.
5. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY: Licensor's aggregate liability shall not exceed $10,000 in any twelve-month period.
6. GOVERNING LAW: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of England and Wales.

output.summary: Two-year non-exclusive software licensing agreement between NovaTech Solutions Ltd. (Licensor, UK) and BrightPath Inc. (Licensee, California). Annual fee of $120,000 with 2% monthly late interest. License is US-only and restricted to internal use. Notable risk: liability cap of $10,000 is significantly lower than annual fee paid ($120,000), and the 10-day cure period for termination is short. Governed by English and Welsh law, which may require specialist counsel for a US-based licensee.

keyTerms.description: The NovaTech Analytics Platform software being licensed
Licensor may grant the same license to other parties simultaneously
2% per month accrued on overdue license fees

obligations.obligation: Restrict use of Software to internal business operations within the United States only
Refrain from sublicensing, modifying, reverse engineering, or creating derivative works
Provide the Software for use under the terms of the license

recommendations: Negotiate the liability cap upward — $10,000 is disproportionate to the $120,000 annual fee and provides inadequate protection if the Software fails
Request a longer cure period (30 days is standard) before Licensor may terminate for breach
Confirm that English and Welsh governing law is acceptable; consider negotiating for California or Delaware law given Licensee's domicile
Calendar the annual fee due date (February 1) to avoid the 2% monthly late interest penalty
Clarify whether 'internal business operations' includes use by contractors or affiliated entities to avoid inadvertent breach

metadata.author: migration
metadata.description: General-purpose review of any agreement type (partnership, settlement, licensing, joint venture, NDA, MOU, etc.) to extract parties, key terms, obligations, and risk assessment
